### SOURCE CITATION FIXES (add/fix URLs)

| # | Claim | Current Source | Issue | Fix |
|---|-------|---------------|-------|-----|
| 3 | F08: "Personalized results improve conversion 15–20% (Forrester Research)" | https://www.forrester.com/ | Homepage does not contain this stat. No specific Forrester report URL provided. Cannot be externally verified. | Option A: Replace with Baymard Institute stat on personalization (they publish verifiable e-commerce benchmarks at baymard.com/research). Option B: Remove the stat entirely. Option C: Replace with a verifiable Algolia-published benchmark. Do NOT present https://www.forrester.com/ as the source for a specific stat — it is the company homepage, not a report URL. |

### STALE DATA (consider noting in deliverables)

| # | Data Point | Audit Value | Current Value | Note |
|---|-----------|------------|---------------|------|
| 4 | Mister-Auto case study framing | "12% conversion lift" | "12% boost in conversions" (from a 2019 A/B test of sort relevance vs. sort relevance + best sellers) | Metric is real and sourced. Recommend adding context: "In a 2019 A/B test, Mister-Auto saw a 12% boost in conversions using Algolia sort relevance + best sellers." This prevents a prospect from asking why the number seems oddly specific. |
